hero.title:

hero.essence: Navigate complex legal terrain with confidence.

credentials: Master of Advanced Studies in International Taxation, Lausanne, Switzerland
about.body: Adv. Dr. Ani Miteva specialises in international tax and financial law. Holding a doctorate from Sofia University and an advanced degree from the University of Lausanne, she combines academic depth with practical precision — finding the right path to the summit of every legal challenge.

practice.title: Legal assistance with precision
practice.subtitle: Specialised counsel in tax, financial and administrative law.
practice.quote: Responsibility — and indeed humanity — demand that we do not exceed our competence, and no lawyer's competence is unlimited

hero.title: Adv. Dr. Ani Miteva
hero.subtitle: Lawyer and lecturer in tax and financial law

bio.title: Biography
bio.p1: Adv. Dr. Ani Miteva is a member of the Sofia Bar Association (since 2018) and Senior Assistant at the Department of Administrative Law Sciences, Faculty of Law, Sofia University "St. Clement of Ohrid" (since 2019).
bio.p2: She specialises in international tax law, financial law and bioethics. She is the author of over 30 academic publications in Bulgarian and international journals, including the monograph "Discrimination in International Tax Law".
bio.p3: She is a member of the International Fiscal Association (IFA), the UNESCO International Bioethics Network and the National Association for Health Policy and Management. She also serves on the Disciplinary Court of the Sofia Bar Association.

position.title: Academic Position
position.body: Since 2019, Ani Miteva has held the position of Senior Assistant at the Department of Administrative Law Sciences, Faculty of Law, Sofia University "St. Clement of Ohrid". She teaches Financial Law and Tax Law.

publications.title: Publications
publications.body: Author of over 30 academic publications in Bulgarian and international peer-reviewed journals. Research interests encompass international tax law, financial law and bioethics.
publications.monographDesc: The first Bulgarian scholarly work addressing tax discrimination. Establishes a definition of 'tax discrimination' grounded in constitutional equal-treatment principles, examines prohibitions against unequal tax treatment in international contexts, and concludes with legislative recommendations for Bulgaria's future treaty negotiations.
